summary_one_line: Input must carry the exact 'CFF ' table tag inside a well-formed sfnt header so the blob resolves to a non-Null OT::cff1 table.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A valid sfnt/OpenType container whose table directory names a 'CFF ' table (bytes 43 46 46 20 at the tag position) with a correctly-formed sfnt version and table offset, so this->blob->as<OT::cff1>() returns a real CFF table rather than the shared Null sentinel.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All four winner-resolving seeds (value_profile_cmplog, trial 1) carry the literal 'CFF ' (43 46 46 20) at offsets 0x0c-0x0f plus the sfnt magic 00 01 00 00 at 0x00-0x03 and a 00 00 00 18 table offset, exactly the byte set that lets the blob resolve to a non-Null OT::cff1 so line 1051 takes its false (winning) branch.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds (value_profile, trial 1) lack the 'CFF ' tag at that offset (W shows 43(C)x4/46(F)x4 there while L shows 47(G)/4f(O)/53(S) and scattered bytes), so the blob never resolves to a CFF table and line 1051 always takes its true branch into { fini (); return; }.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog wins because its I2SRandReplace stage reads the table-tag comparison from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the exact 'CFF ' operand into the input at the tag offset; value_profile alone lacks I2SRandReplace and cannot place the exact 4-byte tag, while cmplog alone fails to bank the partially-correct sfnt header progress, so only the combined fuzzer reaches the false branch.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a pointer-equality gate against the Null OT::cff1 sentinel; the false branch (proceeding into CFF parsing) is the globally blocked side.
Resolving the false branch requires the blob to be interpreted as a real OT::cff1 table.
Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal 'CFF ' tag and sfnt magic in their header bytes.
At the tag offsets 0x0c-0x0f the winner bytes are concentrated on C/F/F/space while the loser bytes differ.
The loser inputs take the true (blocking) branch at line 1051 in every counted trial while the winner takes the false branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1051  T=0 F=4  T=20 F=0  if (cff == &Null (OT::cff1))  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Pair 1 is decided by the value_profile delta (vpc beats cmplog).
Pair 2 is decided by the I2S delta (vpc beats value_profile), so resolving requires both techniques jointly.
I2SRandReplace substitutes a CMP operand into the input at matching offsets, which can place the exact 'CFF ' tag.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile contributes a CMP_MAP gradient that rewards inputs producing closer-to-matching CMP operand pairs, banking partial header progress.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 7170 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 2c9af73dd0cf5b3a` and observed exactly one I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 19 (ancestor e0a5b09f80625fb6), the floor signal for an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current vpc build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gated on the exact 'CFF ' tag where cmplog-alone or value_profile-alone resolves the false branch as fast as value_profile_cmplog, showing neither I2S substitution nor the CMP_MAP gradient is necessary.

weakest_evidence_point: Whether the single depth-19 dash row genuinely placed the 'CFF ' tag versus some other CMP operand cannot be confirmed from lineage alone, since the floor signal only proves an I2S find occurred somewhere in the chain.
